title: news.processed
description: A clean, deduplicated, normalized news item produced by the Go processing consumer group to the Kafka topic `news.processed`. This is the input to the Python inference layer. It is a superset of `news.raw`: same identity fields plus processing metadata (`content_hash`, `processed_at`). Model output (sentiment/entities) still must NOT appear here — that is written to the datastore by Python, not back onto the topic.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Bump on any breaking change so both languages fail loudly on mismatch.

id.description: Same stable unique id carried over from news.raw. Used as the Kafka message key.

content_hash.description: Hash of the normalized content used for dedup in the Go processing stage. Format: `sha256:<64 lowercase hex>`.

feed.description: Optional feed name or identifier, carried over from news.raw.

url.description: Optional canonical URL, carried over from news.raw.

language.description: Optional ISO 639-1 language code, carried over from news.raw.

published_at.description: Original publish time, carried over from news.raw (RFC 3339, UTC).

ingested_at.description: Ingestion time, carried over from news.raw (RFC 3339, UTC).

processed_at.description: Time the Go processing stage produced this item to news.processed (RFC 3339, UTC).

tickers.description: Optional ticker hints carried over from news.raw. Still NOT model output.
